data.title: Market Chatter: Samsung Life Insurance Fully Digitalizes Insurance Services
data.description:
Samsung Life Insurance completely digitalized its insurance transaction process, enabling contract changes, claims, and consultations to be carried out online.

The company, building on its 2020 digital sign-up system, now offers mobile and web access for most services, along with AI chatbots and live chat support, Pulse News reported Tuesday.

New features comprise multi-party video consultations and digital kiosks for in-branch self-service, while proxy transactions are supported via electronic power of attorney, the report said.

Shares of Samsung Life Insurance fell more than 2% in recent trade on Wednesday.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Luxshare Precision Industry Falls 5.1%; ZTE Corp. Down 3.2%
Trump issues order to block state climate change policies
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump issued an executive order on Tuesday that aims to block the enforcement of state laws passed to reduce the use of fossil fuels and combat climate change.

The move is the latest in a string of efforts by Trump's administration to pump up domestic energy output and push back against largely Democratic-led policies to curb carbon emissions. It came just hours after Trump, a Republican, issued orders to increase coal production.

The order directed the U.S. attorney general to identify state laws that address climate change, ESG initiatives, environmental justice and carbon emissions, and to take action to block them.

"Many States have enacted, or are in the process of enacting, burdensome and ideologically motivated 'climate change' or energy policies that threaten American energy dominance and our economic and national security," the order said.

Trump specifically cited laws in New York and Vermont that fine fossil fuel companies for their contribution to climate change, California's cap-and-trade policy, and lawsuits by states that have sought to hold energy companies accountable for their role in global warming.

The two Democratic governors who co-chair the U.S. Climate Alliance - Kathy Hochul of New York and Michelle Lujan Grisham of New Mexico - said states could not be stripped of their authority and would not be deterred by the executive order.

"We will keep advancing solutions to the climate crisis that safeguard Americans' fundamental right to clean air and water, create good-paying jobs, grow the clean energy economy, and make our future healthier and safer,” they said in a joint statement.

The Alliance is a group of 24 governors dedicated to climate action.

The American Petroleum Institute, an oil and gas trade group, praised the order.

"We welcome President Trump's action to hold states like New York and California accountable for pursuing unconstitutional efforts that illegally penalize U.S. oil and natural gas producers for delivering the energy American consumers rely on every day," API Senior Vice President Ryan Meyers said in a statement.

data.title: Panther Minerals Announces Director Appointment
data.description:
(TheNewswire)


Vancouver, British Columbia -
TheNewswire – April
8th, 2025 - Panther Minerals Inc. (“Panther Minerals” or the “Company”) (CSE: PURR) (OTC: GLIOF)
(FWB: 2BC) announces that Mr. Casey Forward has been appointed as a
director of the Company, effective immediately.


Mr. Forward has worked with numerous public and private companies over
the past 30 years, currently including positions as interim CEO of
MegaWatt Lithium and Battery Metals Corp., director of Blast Resources
Corp., and director, CFO of Leanlife Health Inc., and previous
positions as CFO of NioCorp Developments Ltd. Mr. Forward is a
Chartered Professional Accountant and a Certified General Accountant.


Mr. Michael Leahy has resigned from the Board of Directors, effective
immediately. The Company would like to thank Mr. Leahy for his
valuable contributions and dedication during his tenure as a director.
The Company wishes him all the best in his future endeavors.


About Panther Minerals Inc.


Panther Minerals is a mineral exploration company actively involved in
the exploration of its North American project portfolio. The
acquisition of the Boulder Creek option reflects the Company's
continuing intention of pursuing advanced, high-quality prospective
uranium projects that can be readily worked on and efficiently
explored in a timely manner.


ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD OF
DIRECTORS


Mr. Robert Birmingham, Chief Executive Officer


For more information please visit: www.pantherminerals.ca or email info@pantherminerals.ca.

The CSE and Information ServiceProvider have not reviewed and does not accept responsibility for theaccuracy or adequacy of this release.

data.title: New Frontier Minerals Reports Exploration Potential of Northern Territory Heavy Rare Earth Elements Project after Rock Chip Sample Analysis
data.description:
New Frontier Minerals said rare earth distribution analysis of rock chip samples from the Cusp and Bobs prospects at the Harts Range heavy rare earth elements project in the Northern Territory highlighted its exploration potential, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

Analysis of the 25 rock chip samples collected highlighted up to 11.75% grade of dysprosium oxide and 1.87% grade of terbium oxide within the total rare earth element mix, the filing said.

The demand for rare earth elements continues to grow in light of their critical role in various industries, per the filing.

data.title: Australia Private House Permits Continue to Rise
data.description:
Private house approvals in Australia increased by 1% month-on-month to 9,203 units in February 2025, in line with flash data but easing from a 1.4% rise in the previous month.

This marked the second consecutive month of growth, driven by gains in New South Wales (5.1%), Queensland (0.5%), and Victoria (0.3%).

However, declines were recorded in Western Australia (-8.5%) and South Australia (-1.9%).

data.title: Fineos Enters Strategic Partnership With Sutherland for Employee Absence, Leave Management
data.description:
Fineos said Tuesday it entered a strategic partnership with Sutherland for absence and leave management for US employee benefit carriers.

The firm said in a statement that it will integrate its Absence platform, which is built for employee benefits, with Sutherland's expertise in artificial intelligence and automation-driven business-process-as-a-service.

This will help group insurers and administrators efficiently manage employee leaves for their clients, per the company.

Fineos' shares fell nearly 3% in recent trading on Wednesday.

data.title: Regal Partners Funds Under Management Fall 8% in March Quarter; Shares Hit All-Time Low
data.description:
Regal Partners said that its funds under management came in at AU$16.53 billion in the March quarter, down 8.3% compared with AU$18.02 billion in the previous quarter, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

Net flows over the three months to March 31 came in at AU$149 million, with a negative investment performance of AU$540 million.

The company's shares fell nearly 8% in recent Wednesday trade, reaching an all-time low.

data.title: PYC Raises AU$133 Million via Entitlement Offer
data.description:
PYC Therapeutics has raised AU$133 million through its ongoing entitlement offer, part of a broader AU$146 million capital raise, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company expects to receive the remaining AU$13 million by April 30 after the underwriter for the outstanding balance has requested an extension citing liquidity issues, the filing said.

PYC is seeking payment guarantees and a bond from the underwriter to ensure the completion of the transaction, the filing added.

data.title: Counting down, less than 3 hours to go: US Imports from China face 104% tariff at midnight
data.description:
Trump is set to impose a 104% tariff on Chinese goods starting Wednesday at 12.01am US Eastern time.

  • the 104% is serviced from his 545% tariffs on China plus his added 50% in retaliation for China's placing of tariffs on the US

US journalist Charles Gasparino says Trump is pressing ahead due to advice he has received that "the tumult in on Wall Street in stock market, while significant, will not be matched by a similar tumult in the Main Street economy".

data.title: NoviqTech and GRR launch Blockchain-backed sustainability certificates for recycled glycol
data.description: NoviqTech has partnered with Global Resource Recovery (GRR) to mint Proof of Sustainability certificates for glycol recycled by a major Australian LNG operator. Under the terms of the deal, each kilolitre of glycol processed will be permanently recorded on blockchain using Carbon Central, providing real-time auditability and high-quality proof of sustainability claims. The exercise aims to ensure that the operator’s recycled glycol meets the highest standards, demonstrating commitment to a circular economy and setting a precedent for the broader industry. Recycled amines The move to recycle glycol follows NoviqTech’s recent integration of its Carbon Central platform to tokenise recycled amines for GRR using Hedera’s groundbreaking distributed ledger network. The expansion of GRR certification to include amines and glycol marks a significant milestone for scaling sustainability practices in industrial recycling. NoviqTech regards the tokens as a pioneering step in the integration of blockchain technology with sustainable resource management and expects partnerships such as the one with GRR to drive the rapid adoption of blockchain-powered certification across the global energy sector. Sustainability standards Glycol recycling builds on the initial success of the Carbon Central platform at GRR’s Northern Territory operations base and reinforces its role in delivering high levels of transparency, traceability and compliance with global sustainability standards. NoviqTech charges a monthly subscription for access to Carbon Central with an additional fee for each token minted, thereby allowing revenue to scale continually in line with the growth of customer operations. Industry research valued the global circular economy market size at $934.5 billion in 2023 and forecasts it to reach $3,199.5b by 2033, with Asia Pacific to grow the fastest over this period. Doubling down NoviqTech chief executive officer Freddy El Turk said the company was doubling down on its efforts in the global circular economy space, helping industrial recyclers and their clients unlock new value through verifiable carbon reduction and circularity claims. “GRR’s industry expertise and extensive client base makes it the ideal partner to drive this transformation,” he said. “This initiative, backed by the leadership of a major Australian LNG operator, is more than a milestone—it is a signal to the entire sector that verifiable sustainability is the way of the future.”

data.title: Dollar slips on yen and Swissy, yuan hits record low
data.description:
Key points:
  • Safe-havens in demand as equity markets skid again
  • Yen climbs, Swiss franc scales 6-month top on dollar
  • Yuan slides as U.S. set to raise China tariff to 104%

The dollar lost ground to the safe-haven yen and Swiss franc on Wednesday as the imminent imposition by the U.S. of 104% tariffs on China spooked world equity markets and sent the Chinese yuan to record lows.

President Donald Trump showed no sign of backing away from the new increase of 50% on China, which goes into effect in just a few hours, accusing Beijing of manipulating the yuan to offset the levies.

"If that goes ahead, you may as well kiss goodbye to any last, lingering hopes that the U.S. economy might avoid a deep recession," said Chris Weston, head of research at broker Pepperstone.

"The message from the White House appears to be this – the beatings will continue until morale improves."

The dollar has already reached an all-time top on the yuan offshore at 7.4288 , breaching the previous high at 7.3765, and all eyes are on China's central bank to see whether it allows a further easing at its daily fix.

Worries about a U.S. downturn were pressuring the dollar elsewhere as markets returned to pricing in more rate cuts from the Federal Reserve.

Fed fund futures (0#FF:) jumped in early Asian trade to imply around 111 basis points of cuts this year, compared to 92 basis points early on Tuesday. (0#USDIRPR)

Fresh losses in U.S. stock futures and heavy selling in longer-dated U.S. Treasuries sparked demand for safe haven currencies, notably the yen and Swiss franc.

"Our top FX expression is to be long yen given U.S. stagflation fears, exacerbated by Trump's aggressive reciprocal tariffs and tit-for-tat escalations, and the sharp selloff in equities," wrote analysts at Nomura in a note.

"Beyond the yen's relative safe-haven status, Japan's macro backdrop remains relatively strong and rate differentials are expected to continue to favour JPY."

The dollar lost 0.7% to 145.23 yen , heading back toward the recent six-month trough of 144.55. The yen was also up sharply on a range of high-yielding currencies as investors unwound carry trades.

The dollar touched a fresh six-month low on the Swiss franc at 0.8430 , threatening major support at 0.8374.

The euro also edged higher, helped by reports Germany's conservatives had reached a deal with the centre-left Social Democrats to form a government, easing political concerns in the EU's largest economy.

The single currency added 0.4% to $1.0996 , creeping back to last week's peak at $1.1147.
